_note: Pre-baked Mark Manson voice pillar. Same topic as Salo's case so the room can compare voice differentiation directly. Uses Mark's signature moves: labeled idea hook (The Memory Tax), no first-person, antithesis-as-doctrine, clipped imperative close.
topic: Why most AI agents start from zero every session
onlyFounders:
The Memory Tax.

Most people pay it every day without noticing.

Every morning you re-explain yourself. To your team. To your spouse. To the AI you opened ten minutes ago that has no idea who you are.

You accept this when humans do it. You call it "catching up." It's polite.

When a machine does it, you call it broken.

The machine isn't broken. The machine is honest. It's showing you, in compressed time, what your day actually looks like.

Forty minutes of context restoration disguised as work.

The cure isn't a smarter machine. It's a habit.

You write things down once. You feed them back in. You stop trusting your own ability to remember why a decision mattered six months ago.

Most people won't do this. The machine starts over. You start over. Both of you do it again tomorrow.

The trade-off of a fresh slate is that nothing compounds.

The trade-off of a long memory is that you have to be honest about what was in it.

Pick one.
